question: A reaction consumes $37.66~\text{g}$ of reactant A ($M = 32~\text{g/mol}$) to produce product B ($M = 53~\text{g/mol}$) in a $1:1$ molar ratio. Calculate the theoretical yield of product B in grams.
explanation: Moles of A $n = \frac{37.66}{32} = 1.18~\text{mol}$. Since ratio is $1:1$, moles of B $= 1.18$. Mass of B $m = n \times M_B = (1.18)(53) = 62.37~\text{g}$.
